2606.07219.title: Adversarial Creation and Detection of AI-Generated Social Bot Content
2606.07219.paper_summary: The convergence of large language models and social bots allows malicious actors to manipulate the information ecosystem by generating human-like content at scale. Existing models for detecting AI-generated content often fail in the wild, primarily due to the lack of ground-truth data. We address this gap through an adversarial methodology that models the impersonation of real social media users by malicious actors. Using this methodology, we curate a multilingual, cross-platform dataset of paired human and AI-generated messages. Training on such adversarial data yields accurate detection of AI-generated text. Our approach significantly outperforms existing models for content-based bot detection in real-world, out-of-distribution data.
2606.07219.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型與社交機器人的融合使惡意行為者能夠通過大規模生成類人內容來操縱信息生態系統。現有的檢測AI生成內容的模型在實際應用中往往失敗，主要是因為缺乏真實數據。我們通過一種對抗性方法來解決這一問題，該方法模擬惡意行為者對真實社交媒體用戶的冒充。利用這種方法，我們策劃了一個多語言、跨平台的數據集，該數據集包含配對的人工和AI生成的消息。對這種對抗性數據進行訓練可以準確檢測AI生成的文本。我們的方法在現實世界中，對於分佈外數據的基於內容的機器人檢測顯著優於現有模型。
2606.07219.author: Mykola Trokhymovych et.al.
2606.07219.authors: Mykola Trokhymovych, Ricardo Baeza-Yates, Alessandro Flammini, Diego Saez-Trumper, Filippo Menczer
